     The Numbers Business Report

Georgia Rule (2007)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $18,882,880Details
International Box Office $1,936,721
Worldwide Box Office $20,819,601
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $20,227,071
Total $20,227,071
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Teenage Rachel is a real thorn in the side of her mother, Lilly. More than just rebellious, she is incorrigible. After Rachel crashes a car, Lilly decides to haul the girl off to a place to which she vowed never to return: Her own mother’s Idaho home. Georgia, the family matriarch, lives by a strict code and expects all under her roof to do the same. Given structure and purpose, Rachel’s anger at the world begins to subside.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,773,870 (35.9% of total gross)
Legs:2.79 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:90.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,523 opening theaters/2,531 max. theaters, 3.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $32,825,476

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 11th, 2007 (Wide) by Universal
Video Release: September 4th, 2007 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for sexual content and some language.
Running Time: 113 minutes
Plot point: Addiction, Child Abuse, Dysfunctional Family, Relationships Gone Wrong, Sex Crimes
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Morgan Creek Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Teenage Rachel is a real thorn in the side of her mother, Lilly. More than just rebellious, she is incorrigible. After Rachel crashes a car, Lilly decides to haul the girl off to a place to which she vowed never to return: Her own mother’s Idaho home. Georgia, the family matriarch, lives by a strict code and expects all under her roof to do the same. Given structure and purpose, Rachel’s anger at the world begins to subside.
